exercises.title: Class Methods as Factory Constructors
exercises.description: Add classmethod constructors to a Temperature class so it can be created from Fahrenheit or Kelvin, in addition to the default Celsius.
exercises.starterCode:
class Temperature:
    def __init__(self, celsius):
        self.celsius = celsius

    @classmethod
    def from_fahrenheit(cls, fahrenheit):
        # TODO: Convert fahrenheit to celsius: (f - 32) * 5/9
        # TODO: Return cls(celsius_value)
        pass

    @classmethod
    def from_kelvin(cls, kelvin):
        # TODO: Convert kelvin to celsius: kelvin - 273.15
        # TODO: Return cls(celsius_value)
        pass

    @staticmethod
    def is_valid_celsius(value):
        # TODO: Return True if value >= -273.15 (absolute zero)
        pass

    def display(self):
        # TODO: Print: "25.00°C"
        pass


# TODO: Create t1 from Celsius 25, call display()
t1 = Temperature(25)
t1.display()

# TODO: Create t2 from Fahrenheit 212, call display()
t2 = Temperature.from_fahrenheit(212)
t2.display()

# TODO: Create t3 from Kelvin 300, call display()
t3 = Temperature.from_kelvin(300)
t3.display()

# TODO: Print whether -300 is a valid Celsius value
print(Temperature.is_valid_celsius(-300))

exercises.title: Mutable Class Attribute Danger and Fix
exercises.description: Demonstrate the classic mutable class attribute bug, then fix it. Show that using a mutable list as a class attribute causes unintended sharing between instances.
exercises.starterCode:
# BUGGY version - shared list
class StudentBuggy:
    grades = []  # BUG: shared by ALL instances!

    def __init__(self, name):
        self.name = name

    def add_grade(self, g):
        self.grades.append(g)


# FIXED version - instance list
class StudentFixed:
    def __init__(self, name):
        self.name = name
        # TODO: Set self.grades = [] so each instance has its own list
        pass

    def add_grade(self, g):
        # TODO: Append to self.grades
        pass


# Demonstrate the bug
buggy1 = StudentBuggy("Alice")
buggy2 = StudentBuggy("Bob")
buggy1.add_grade(90)
print(f"Bug - Bob's grades after Alice added one: {buggy2.grades}")

# Demonstrate the fix
fixed1 = StudentFixed("Alice")
fixed2 = StudentFixed("Bob")
fixed1.add_grade(90)
print(f"Fix - Bob's grades after Alice added one: {fixed2.grades}")
exercises.expectedOutput:
Bug - Bob's grades after Alice added one: [90]
Fix - Bob's grades after Alice added one: []
hints: The bug: buggy1 and buggy2 share the same grades list because it's a class attribute
The fix: in __init__, set self.grades = [] to create a new list per instance
Never use mutable types (list, dict, set) as class attributes unless sharing is intentional

exercises.title: Static Method Validator
exercises.description: Add static methods to a User class for validation. Static methods belong to the class logically but don't need access to the instance or class.
exercises.expectedOutput:
Users created: 2
Error: Invalid username: ab
Error: Invalid email: notemail
hints: is_valid_username: len 3-20 and all(c.isalnum() or c == '_' for c in username)
is_valid_email: '@' in email and '.' in email.split('@')[-1]

warmup.title: Quick Recall: Class vs Instance Attribute Access
warmup.description: What does this code print? Trace the attribute lookup order.
warmup.starterCode:
class Config:
    debug = False
    version = "1.0"

c1 = Config()
c2 = Config()
c1.debug = True  # Creates instance attribute on c1
print(c1.debug)     # Instance attribute
print(c2.debug)     # Still class attribute
print(Config.debug) # Class attribute
print(c1.version)   # Inherited from class
